title: Sakana Fugu (Fully Tested - V/S Fable): UHM... REALLY?
summary: Sakana Fugu is a multi-agent orchestration system presented as an alternative to models like Fable 5 and Mythos Preview. It uses a learned router to coordinate multiple agents and comes in two versions (standard and Ultra) with varying performance-to-cost tradeoffs. Real-world testing shows strong benchmark performance but mixed practical results on coding and visual generation tasks.
writeup: The video tests Sakana Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system built around a learned router, available as Fugu and Fugu Ultra. It compares the system against Fable 5 across benchmarks and hands-on tasks. Fugu posts strong results on Terminal Bench, LiveCodeBench, and GPQA Diamond, but trails Fable 5 on SWE Bench Pro and Humanity's Last Exam, with mixed outcomes in practical coding and visual generation. The takeaway is that benchmark strength does not translate uniformly, since model routing helps on some workloads while leaving practical coding and visual results inconsistent.
